name: Bath Academy
subhead: Small day and boarding college in central Bath focused on GCSE and A-level pathways, with personalised academic support and a compact sixth form.
metaDescription: Small day and boarding college in central Bath focused on GCSE and A-level pathways, with personalised academic support and a compact sixth form.

Parents Like

  • Its scale feels very different from the larger Bath schools.
  • Families looking for a more personalised sixth-form route often notice it early.
  • The city-centre location is practical for older students.

How the School Describes Itself

  • Bath Academy describes itself as highly personalised and tailored to individual need.
  • Its language focuses on academic support, flexibility and outcomes.
  • The school also positions itself as international in outlook.

Fee Profile

Annual fees including VAT.

Year 10£27,060
Year 11£27,060
Year 12£27,060
Year 13£27,060

Bursaries

Bath Academy’s official materials do not set out a formal published bursary scheme or means-tested criteria. The school says scholarships may be offered to students who show academic potential, and that requests for scholarships or bursaries can be discussed during the application stage. Families interested in financial support need to raise this directly with admissions.

subhead: Co-educational day and boarding school near Radstock, included in the Bath guide for families willing to look beyond the city itself.

Parents Like

  • It offers a much smaller scale than several Bath competitors.
  • Boarding is central to the feel of the school rather than an add-on.
  • Families sometimes shortlist it when they want a Bath-area option with a stronger boarding identity.

How the School Describes Itself

  • Downside tends to describe itself as academically serious, values-led and strongly community-focused.
  • Its Catholic heritage is a visible part of how it presents the school.
  • The school also leans into depth of pastoral relationships and a broad boarding life.

Bursaries

Downside says its means-tested bursaries are available to pupils entering the school at all age levels and can also support current families whose financial circumstances change unexpectedly. The school’s bursary fund page says around one-third of pupils receive financial assistance, with awards ranging from 10% to fully funded transformational places and requiring around £2 million a year. Applications are handled in strict confidence and reviewed annually.

name: King Edward's School
subhead: All-through day school in central Bath, with nursery-to-sixth-form continuity, strong academic breadth and a broad co-curricular programme.
metaDescription: All-through day school in central Bath, with nursery-to-sixth-form continuity, strong academic breadth and a broad co-curricular programme.
featureTilesHtml:
A-level Results

% A*–A62%
% A*–B90%

Classics1%
Design & technology1%
Religious studies1%

Scholarships

For the move into Year 7, applicants from both King Edward's Junior School and external schools may be considered for a scholarship and/or award. Special Talent Awards are available in art, drama, music and sport.

Inspection Snapshot

In the January 2023 ISI inspection, the school met the required standards and the quality of pupils' academic and other achievements and personal development were both judged excellent.

Assessment Approach

King Edward's uses its own assessment framework across the junior school; ISI said in 2023 that this framework showed pupils making good progress. In pre-prep, the school records a Reception baseline, tracks progress through the year and uses daily formative assessment to shape next steps.

Parents Like

  • One site from nursery through sixth form makes the journey feel coherent.
  • The city-centre setting is unusually practical for Bath families.
  • The sixth form has enough breadth to keep both humanities and STEM options open.

How the School Describes Itself

  • The school presents itself as ambitious, broad-minded and energetic rather than narrowly selective.
  • It stresses academic, sporting and creative excellence in equal measure.
  • Its own language leans heavily on curiosity, discovery and adventure.

Fee Profile

Annual fees including VAT.

Reception£14,454
Year 1£14,454
Year 2£14,454

Year 5£17,208

Year 10£21,762

Year 12£22,140
Year 13£22,140

Bursaries

King Edward’s does not offer financial assistance for entry to the Junior School. Means-tested bursaries are available for Year 7 and Year 12 entrants, and the school also offers hardship/emergency bursaries for current Senior School families whose circumstances change. In 2025–26 KES says it provided almost £1.3 million in bursary support to more than 100 Senior School pupils, with many Year 7 awards covering 40% to 100% of fees and up to 100% available.

name: Kingswood Preparatory School
subhead: Prep school in Lansdown with a linked senior-school route, broad co-curricular life and day-only format from nursery through Year 6.
metaDescription: Prep school in Lansdown with a linked senior-school route, broad co-curricular life and day-only format from nursery through Year 6.
featureTilesHtml:
Leavers Destinations

Kingswood Prep is the linked junior school within the Kingswood Foundation. Published inspection material says most pupils transfer to Kingswood Senior School.

Scholarships

Published inspection material says several prep leavers have secured scholarships in music, design and technology, drama and sport.

Inspection Snapshot

In the May 2024 ISI report, the prep school met the independent school standards, with inspectors highlighting a broad curriculum, good progress and strong safeguarding. The same inspection found that the separately registered early-years childcare provision, The Garden, did not consistently meet all childcare requirements.

Assessment Approach

Kingswood says it tracks progress through annual national standardised tests in English (PTE), mathematics (PTM) and general ability (CATs), plus a termly NGRT reading test and half-termly internal English and maths assessments.

Parents Like

  • Families often value the direct link into Kingswood Senior School.
  • It offers a genuine prep-school atmosphere without needing boarding.
  • The Lansdown setting feels separate from the city while remaining close to Bath.

How the School Describes Itself

  • The school describes itself as part of the wider Kingswood Foundation rather than a standalone prep.
  • It places emphasis on breadth, confidence and a rounded junior-school experience.
  • Published material also leans into continuity with the senior school.

Fee Profile

Annual fees including VAT.

Reception£15,108
Year 1£15,108
Year 2£15,108
Year 3£18,060
Year 4£18,060
Year 5£18,342
Year 6£18,342

Bursaries

Kingswood says bursaries are not available for children attending Kingswood Preparatory School unless there are very exceptional circumstances. Its published bursary scheme is otherwise aimed at Senior School entry in Years 7, 9 and 12. No standard Prep bursary tariff or application route is published online.

Kingswood offers means-tested bursaries for pupils joining Years 7, 9 and 12. The school says support ranges from 10% to over 100% of fees and that bursary holders are spread across every Senior School year, including day, flexi-boarding and boarding places. Families are encouraged to discuss eligibility early, before formal assessment.

name: Monkton Combe School
subhead: All-through day and boarding school just outside Bath, with a countryside setting and a sixth form notable for art, sciences and broad subject choice.
metaDescription: All-through day and boarding school just outside Bath, with a countryside setting and a sixth form notable for art, sciences and broad subject choice.

Scholarships

Monkton offers scholarships for academic, sporting, artistic and leadership potential. Successful applicants can receive up to 20% off day fees, while the Monktonian scholarship offers 10% off full boarding.

Inspection Snapshot

Monkton's published ISI highlights from May 2024 say the school passed inspection in all areas. The school highlights a broad curriculum, typically good progress, strong pastoral systems and rigorous safeguarding, with next-step recommendations around lesson planning and further embedding financial literacy.

Assessment Approach

Monkton says assessment data is tracked and analysed by leaders, managers and teachers so that pupils receive the right support and challenge and make typically good progress. One-to-one tutoring and targeted support form part of the wider prep support structure.

Parents Like

  • The setting feels more rural and self-contained than most Bath options.
  • Families looking for boarding flexibility often shortlist Monkton early.
  • Its A-level profile is notably broad, with art unusually prominent.

How the School Describes Itself

  • Monkton presents itself as energetic, values-led and highly relational.
  • It talks about character, purpose and the development of the whole person.
  • Published material also leans into adventure, leadership and broad opportunity.

Monkton says it offers bursaries alongside scholarships to support families who require financial assistance. Its public admissions pages do not currently publish a separate bursary policy, award range or formal eligibility thresholds online, but they do state that scholarship holders are looked on favourably when bursaries are awarded. The school also advertises separate discounts for military families.

name: Paragon School
subhead: Prep school in Bath with a linked route into Prior Park College, a broad curriculum and a strong emphasis on small classes.
metaDescription: Prep school in Bath with a linked route into Prior Park College, a broad curriculum and a strong emphasis on small classes.
featureTilesHtml:
Leavers Destinations

The Paragon is the junior school of Prior Park College. In 2025, the school said 72% of its Year 6 cohort would continue to Prior Park College, and it presents leavers' destinations as part of an annual secondary-destination update.

Scholarships

Paragon highlights awards and scholarships won by its Year 6 leavers at destination senior schools, and links these alongside destinations in its recent end-of-year leavers' coverage.

Inspection Snapshot

In the June 2025 ISI report, all the relevant standards were met. Inspectors highlighted a comprehensive curriculum, good progress including for pupils with SEND and EAL, and effective safeguarding, while recommending more consistent use of assessment data across all subjects.

Assessment Approach

Paragon's 2025 ISI report says English and maths are monitored through regular assessments, including standardised tests and teacher-led evaluations, with termly pupil progress meetings used to identify where pupils need extra support or more challenge.

Parents Like

  • It gives families a prep option inside the Prior Park family of schools.
  • The smaller size can feel more personal than some Bath rivals.
  • Its setting and access to Prior Park facilities stand out at this age range.

How the School Describes Itself

  • The school describes itself as broad, warm and academically ambitious.
  • Published language repeatedly highlights small classes and specialist teaching.
  • It also leans on its woodland setting and close relationship with Prior Park College.

Fee Profile

Annual fees including VAT.

Reception£14,630
Year 1£14,630
Year 2£14,630
Year 3£16,272
Year 4£16,272
Year 5£16,682
Year 6£16,682

Bursaries

Paragon’s fees page says bursaries are awarded under the Prior Park Schools means-tested policy in response to parental need, either to relieve difficulty for an existing Paragon family or to support entry to Prior Park College. The 2025 admissions policy adds that bursaries for new entrants to The Paragon are not generally considered. Any award replaces other discounts and is reviewed as family circumstances change.
address: Lyncombe House, Lyncombe Vale, Bath, BA2 4LT

Bursaries

Royal High Bath offers GDST means-tested bursaries for girls entering Year 7, external Year 9 and Sixth Form. Awards are for students with strong all-round academic ability whose families cannot cover the full fees; both income and assets are assessed, and bursaries are reviewed annually. The school says around 9% of pupils currently receive financial assistance, with all or part of fees paid.
